title: PM Idempotency Per-Route Ops Breakdown
objective: Implement the smallest bounded follow-on to packet 019h by adding one additional read-only PM ops surface at `GET /api/v1/ops/pm-idempotency/by-route` that reports per-route idempotency inventory and expiry pressure for the existing seven PM POST handlers, while preserving the packet-019 through packet-019h request contract, keeping the work schema registry at 22, keeping `/api/v1/work/*` path count at 15, limiting `/api/v1/ops/*` growth to one new GET endpoint, and avoiding any DDL, any PM write-surface expansion, or any `work.wbs_nodes` writes.
active_role: PM Idempotency Per-Route Ops Breakdown Author
constrained_outputs: One bounded read-only PM ops endpoint at `GET /api/v1/ops/pm-idempotency/by-route` returning `{by_route: [{route, count, expired_count, oldest_expires_at}, ...], backend_kind}` for the existing seven PM POST handlers only
One minimal backend extension on the existing idempotency seam that computes per-route counts and expiry pressure without changing the durable-store schema or introducing a second observability path

Focused regression evidence proving work schema registry size stays at 22, `/api/v1/work/*` path count stays at 15, `/api/v1/ops/*` path count grows only from 1 to 2, and all ops routes remain GET-only

A bounded handoff naming the next smallest follow-on after per-route observability, including the alternative of migrating the scheduled sweep to Kubernetes only when a legitimate deployment-manifests lane exists
validation_steps: Confirm packet 019i stays bounded to per-route read-only PM idempotency observability and does not add any PM write endpoint, any broader entity write surface, or any `work.wbs_nodes` write
Confirm the new payload is derived from the existing packet-019g stats seam and keeps the durable and in-memory backend contract aligned without authoring DDL or changing `pm.idempotency_keys` shape

Confirm the per-route breakdown remains pinned to the seven existing PM POST routes and does not widen `/api/v1/work/*` or alter the packet-019 through packet-019h idempotency request contract, replay behavior, or mismatch 422 shape

Confirm packet-010a, packet-010b, packet-011f, packet-013, packet-013i, packet-013j, packet-014, packet-014i, packet-015, packet-015i, packet-016, packet-017, packet-018, packet-019, packet-019f, packet-019g, and packet-019h surfaces still pass or remain unchanged as expected after the per-route observability work
sql_permission_note: Packet 019i is runtime observability only. No new SQL, schema changes, migration edits, PM write-surface expansion, or `work.wbs_nodes` writes are authorized.
